1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

9	/-!
10	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
11	
12	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
14	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
15	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
16	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
17	
18	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
19	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
20	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
21	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
22	
23	1. a binary tensor-product operation
24	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
25	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
26	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
27	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
28	
29	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
30	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
31	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
32	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
33	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
34	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
35	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
36	
37	## Status (current)
38	
39	`tensorObj` and `tensorObj_functoriality` are fully defined (no `sorry`), lifting
40	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through sheafification on the small Zariski
41	site. The remaining typed-`sorry` residuals are the `⊗`-inverse lane
42	(`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`) plus the route-(e)
43	whiskering residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`; the dual block
44	(`InternalHom.internalHom`, `dual`, `evalLin`/`internalHomEvalApp`) is built
45	axiom-clean toward closing them. Once the inverse lands, the consumer
46	`PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` closes the residual `addCommGroup`
47	sorry of `RelPicFunctor.lean` L235.
48	
49	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
50	
51	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (def) — the substrate binary
52	   operation `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`,
53	   lifting `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` on underlying presheaves
54	   composed with sheafification on the small Zariski site.
55	   Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`.
56	
57	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality` (def) — the
58	   functorial action of `⊗` on morphisms: a pair `f : M ⟶ M'`, `g : N ⟶ N'`
59	   determines `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`.
60	   Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`.
61	
62	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.monoidalCategory` (instance) — the
63	   monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X` with tensor `⊗`, unit
64	   `O_X`, associator, unitors, and braiding inherited from
65	   `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal` under sheafification.
66	   Per blueprint `thm:scheme_modules_monoidal`.
67	
68	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (def) — the
69	   `AddCommGroup` structure on the relative Picard quotient
70	   `Quotient (RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup πC πT)`, built via the
71	   `tensorObj` substrate. This is the iter-204+ closure target for the Lane RPF
72	   L235/L266-269 `addCommGroup` residual sorry.
73	   Per blueprint `thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj`.
74	
75	Plus (PUSH-BEYOND) the supporting helper lemmas of the lift section
76	(`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`,
77	`lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible`, `lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct`,
78	`lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj`).

96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — base change along a ring iso commutes
97	with `⊗` (the H2 "bottom gap" of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`)
98	
99	For a *ring isomorphism* `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `A`, `B`, base change along
100	`e` (giving each `S`-module its `R`-module structure via `Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom`)
101	commutes with the tensor product: the canonical map `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b` is an
102	`R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B`. Equivalently, `restrictScalars`
103	along a ring iso is *strong* monoidal — the lax tensorator is invertible. Mathlib
104	has `ModuleCat.extendScalars` strong monoidal but `restrictScalars` only
105	`LaxMonoidal`; this ring-iso strong upgrade is absent and is the documented "REAL
106	bottom gap" (H2) of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
107	
108	section RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
109	
110	open TensorProduct
111	
112	variable {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
113	
114	/-- The `R`-linear equivalence `A ⊗[R] B ≃ₗ[R] A ⊗[S] B` (`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ b`),
115	where the `R`-module structures are base-changed along the ring iso `e : R ≃+* S`.
116	Base change along a ring iso commutes with `⊗`. -/
117	noncomputable def restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv (e : R ≃+* S)
118	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] :
119	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
120	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
121	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
122	    TensorProduct R A B ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B := by
123	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
124	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
125	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
126	  -- Forward: `a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`, an `R`-bilinear-to-linear lift.
127	  let fwd : TensorProduct R A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct S A B :=
128	    TensorProduct.lift
129	      { toFun := fun a =>
130	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[S] b
131	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add]
132	            map_smul' := fun r b => by
133	              simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
134	              change a ⊗ₜ[S] (e r • b) = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
135	              rw [TensorProduct.tmul_smul] }
136	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul]
137	        map_smul' := fun r a => by
138	          ext b
139	          simp only [RingHom.id_apply, LinearMap.smul_apply, LinearMap.coe_mk,
140	            AddHom.coe_mk]
141	          change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[S] b = e r • (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)
142	          rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul', TensorProduct.smul_tmul] }
143	  -- Inverse: `a ⊗ₜ[S] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[R] b`. Built as an additive lift out of the
144	  -- `S`-tensor (scalar-swap compatibility uses `s • a = e.symm s •ᵣ a`), then
145	  -- shown `R`-linear (`R` acting on the `S`-tensor via `e`).
146	  let bwdAdd : TensorProduct S A B →+ TensorProduct R A B :=
147	    TensorProduct.liftAddHom
148	      { toFun := fun a =>
149	          { toFun := fun b => a ⊗ₜ[R] b
150	            map_zero' := by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
151	            map_add' := fun b b' => by rw [TensorProduct.tmul_add] }
152	        map_zero' := by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.zero_tmul]
153	        map_add' := fun a a' => by ext b; simp [TensorProduct.add_tmul] }
154	      (fun s a b => by
155	        simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
156	        -- `(s • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = a ⊗ₜ[R] (s • b)`; move the `S`-scalar through `e.symm`.
157	        have hsa : (s • a) = (e.symm s : R) • a := by
158	          change s • a = e (e.symm s) • a; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
159	        have hsb : (s • b) = (e.symm s : R) • b := by
160	          change s • b = e (e.symm s) • b; rw [e.apply_symm_apply]
161	        rw [hsa, hsb]; exact TensorProduct.smul_tmul _ _ _ )
162	  let bwd : TensorProduct S A B →ₗ[R] TensorProduct R A B :=
163	    { toFun := bwdAdd
164	      map_add' := bwdAdd.map_add
165	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
166	        simp only [RingHom.id_apply]
167	        -- `R` acts on the `S`-tensor via `e`; reduce to additive `S`-scalar action.
168	        change bwdAdd (e r • x) = r • bwdAdd x
169	        induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
170	        | zero => simp
171	        | tmul a b =>
172	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
173	            change (e r • a) ⊗ₜ[R] b = r • (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)
174	            rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
175	            rfl
176	        | add x y hx hy =>
177	            rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, hx, hy, smul_add] }
178	  refine LinearEquiv.ofLinear fwd bwd ?_ ?_
179	  · -- right inverse `fwd ∘ bwd = id`. The composite is `R`-linear over the
180	    -- `S`-tensor, so check on additive generators by induction.
181	    refine LinearMap.ext fun x => ?_
182	    simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
183	    induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
184	    | zero => simp
185	    | tmul a b =>
186	        change fwd (bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
187	        change fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b
188	        simp only [fwd, TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
189	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add bwd, map_add fwd, hx, hy]
190	  · -- left inverse `bwd ∘ fwd = id` on `a ⊗ₜ[R] b` (composite `R`-linear over the
191	    -- `R`-tensor, so `TensorProduct.ext'` applies).
192	    refine TensorProduct.ext' fun a b => ?_
193	    change bwdAdd (fwd (a ⊗ₜ[R] b)) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
194	    change bwdAdd (a ⊗ₜ[S] b) = a ⊗ₜ[R] b
195	    rfl
196	
197	/-- The forward map of `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` on a simple tensor:
198	`a ⊗ₜ[R] b ↦ a ⊗ₜ[S] b`. -/
199	@[simp] lemma restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul (e : R ≃+* S)
200	    (A B : Type u) [AddCommGroup A] [Module S A] [AddCommGroup B] [Module S B] (a : A) (b : B) :
201	    letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
202	    letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
203	    letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
204	    restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e A B (a ⊗ₜ[R] b) = a ⊗ₜ[S] b := by
205	  letI _iA : Module R A := Module.compHom A e.toRingHom
206	  letI _iB : Module R B := Module.compHom B e.toRingHom
207	  letI _iT : Module R (TensorProduct S A B) := Module.compHom _ e.toRingHom
208	  simp only [restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv, LinearEquiv.ofLinear_apply,
209	    TensorProduct.lift.tmul, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk]
210	
211	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is strong monoidal: the
212	lax tensorator `μ` is an isomorphism.** This is the "REAL bottom gap" (H2) of
213	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`. For a ring iso `e : R ≃+* S` and `S`-modules `M₁, M₂`,
214	the underlying map of the lax tensorator
215	`μ : restrictScalars(M₁) ⊗_R restrictScalars(M₂) ⟶ restrictScalars(M₁ ⊗_S M₂)`
216	sends `m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ ↦ m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul`), which is exactly
217	the forward `R`-linear equivalence `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e`, hence is
218	bijective, hence an isomorphism. Mathlib has `extendScalars` strong monoidal but
219	only `restrictScalars` lax; this ring-iso strong upgrade is the documented absent
220	ingredient. -/
221	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ (e : R ≃+* S) (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
222	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) := by
223	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
224	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂)
225	      = ⇑(restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂) := by
226	    ext z
227	    induction z using TensorProduct.induction_on with
228	    | zero => rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm
229	    | tmul a b =>
230	        erw [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul]
231	        exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv_apply_tmul e M₁ M₂ a b).symm
232	    | add x y hx hy => rw [map_add, hx, hy]; exact (map_add _ x y).symm
233	  rw [hfun]
234	  exact (restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv e M₁ M₂).bijective
235	
236	/-- **The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along a ring iso is an
237	isomorphism.** Its underlying map is the ring map `e` (`ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η`),
238	which is bijective since `e` is a ring equivalence. -/
239	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε (e : R ≃+* S) :
240	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) := by
241	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
242	  have hfun : ⇑(Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) = ⇑e := by
243	    ext r
244	    exact ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η (f := e.toRingHom) r
245	  rw [hfun]
246	  exact e.bijective
247	
248	/-- **`ModuleCat.restrictScalars` along a ring isomorphism is (strong) monoidal.**
249	The packaged `Functor.Monoidal` structure obtained from the lax structure by
250	inverting `ε` (`restrictScalars_isIso_ε`) and `μ` (`restrictScalars_isIso_μ`). This
251	is the clean, reusable strong-monoidal upgrade that Mathlib provides for
252	`extendScalars` but not `restrictScalars`; it is the ModuleCat-level core of the H2
253	ingredient of `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
254	@[implicit_reducible]
255	noncomputable def restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv (e : R ≃+* S) :
256	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom).Monoidal := by
257	  haveI : IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom)) :=
258	    restrictScalars_isIso_ε e
259	  haveI : ∀ M₁ M₂, IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars e.toRingHom) M₁ M₂) :=
260	    fun M₁ M₂ => restrictScalars_isIso_μ e M₁ M₂
261	  exact Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal _
262	
263	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal tensorator.** For an arbitrary
264	*bijective* ring hom `f : R →+* S`, the lax tensorator of `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f`
265	is an isomorphism. This is the form consumed by the presheaf-level lift, where the
266	componentwise ring map `(α.app X).hom` of a `NatIso` of ring presheaves is bijective
267	but not literally presented as `(_ : R ≃+* S).toRingHom`. -/
268	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f)
269	    (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat.{u} S) :
270	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f) M₁ M₂) := by
271	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
272	  rw [hfe]
273	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_μ (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf) M₁ M₂
274	
275	/-- **Bijective-ring-hom form of the strong-monoidal unit.** Companion of
276	`restrictScalars_isIso_μ_of_bijective` for the lax unit `ε`. -/
277	lemma restrictScalars_isIso_ε_of_bijective (f : R →+* S) (hf : Function.Bijective f) :
278	    IsIso (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars f)) := by
279	  have hfe : f = (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf).toRingHom := RingHom.ext fun _ => rfl
280	  rw [hfe]
281	  exact restrictScalars_isIso_ε (RingEquiv.ofBijective f hf)
282	
283	end RestrictScalarsRingIsoTensor
284	
285	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal
286	
287	The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
288	presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
289	fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
290	(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
291	the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
292	(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
293	the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
294	close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/
295	
296	namespace PresheafOfModules
297	
298	universe v'
299	
300	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
301	
302	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
303	/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
304	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
305	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
306	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
307	    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
308	      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
309	  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
310	  naturality {X Y} f := by
311	    ext r
312	    dsimp
313	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η,
314	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
315	    simp only [map_one]
316	    erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one]
317	
318	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
319	/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
320	from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
321	noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
322	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
323	    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
324	    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
325	      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
326	  app X := by
327	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
328	  naturality {X Y} f := by
329	    refine ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ ?_)
330	    dsimp
331	    erw [PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul, ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul,
332	      ModuleCat.restrictScalars_μ_tmul, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
333	    rfl
334	
335	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
336	/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
337	commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
338	noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
339	    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
340	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
341	  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
342	  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
343	  μ_natural_left := by
344	    intro X Y f X'
345	    ext1 Z
346	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
347	      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
348	  μ_natural_right := by
349	    intro X Y X' f
350	    ext1 Z
351	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
352	      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
353	  associativity := by
354	    intro M N P
355	    ext1 Z
356	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
357	      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
358	  left_unitality := by
359	    intro M
360	    ext1 Z
361	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
362	      (M.obj Z)
363	  right_unitality := by
364	    intro M
365	    ext1 Z
366	    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
367	      (M.obj Z)

369	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flat left-whiskering preserves the
370	sheafification localizer
371	
372	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
373	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
374	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
375	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
376	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
377	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
378	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
379	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
380	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
381	structure. -/
382	
383	section FlatWhisker
384	
385	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
386	
387	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
388	
389	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
390	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
391	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
392	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
393	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
394	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
395	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
396	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
397	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
398	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
399	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
400	
401	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
402	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
403	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
404	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
405	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
406	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
407	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
408	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
409	
410	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
411	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
412	half. -/
413	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
414	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
415	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
416	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
417	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
418	  constructor
419	  intro U s
420	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
421	  | zero =>
422	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
423	      intro V i _
424	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
425	  | tmul a b =>
426	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
427	      intro V i hi
428	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
429	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
430	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
431	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
432	      congr 1
433	  | add s t hs ht =>
434	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
435	      intro V i hi
436	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
437	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
438	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
439	
440	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
441	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
442	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
443	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
444	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
445	injectivity half. -/
446	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
447	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
448	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
449	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
450	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
451	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
452	  constructor
453	  intro X ξ η h
454	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
455	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
456	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
457	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
458	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
459	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
460	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
461	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
462	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
463	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
464	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
465	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
466	  have hex : Function.Exact
467	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
468	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
469	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
470	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
471	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
472	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
473	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
474	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
475	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
476	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
477	    intro ζ
478	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
479	    | zero =>
480	        rw [map_zero]
481	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
482	    | tmul a kk =>
483	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
484	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
485	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
486	          rw [map_zero]
487	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
488	          exact kk.2
489	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
490	        intro V f hf
491	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
492	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
493	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
494	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
495	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
496	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
497	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
498	        rw [map_add]
499	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
500	        intro V f hf
501	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
502	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
503	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
504	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
505	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
506	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
507	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
508	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
509	  intro V f hf
510	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
511	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
512	  exact hf
513	
514	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
515	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
516	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
517	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
518	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
519	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
520	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
521	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
522	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
523	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
524	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
525	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
526	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
527	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
528	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
529	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
530	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
531	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
532	
533	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
534	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
535	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
536	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
537	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
538	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
539	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
540	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
541	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
542	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
543	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
544	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
545	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
546	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
547	  have hconj : g ▷ F
548	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
549	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
550	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
551	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
552	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
553	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
554	  exact hwl
555	
556	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
557	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
558	
559	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE
560	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible
561	sheaves over non-affine opens and is therefore OFF the associator critical path
562	(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT
563	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
564	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves
565	local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
566	⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on
567	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
568	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
569	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
570	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
571	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/
572	
573	section WhiskerOfW
574	
575	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
576	
577	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
578	
579	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
580	(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
581	whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
582	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.
583	
584	This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
585	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
586	`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
587	is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
588	characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
589	tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
590	stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
591	needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.
592	
593	The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
594	(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
595	(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
596	presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
597	preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
598	`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
599	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
600	here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against
601	it; see the task result for the precise missing Mathlib statements. -/
602	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
603	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
604	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
605	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
606	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
607	  -- ROUTE (e) residual: `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` is an iso since `g_x` is
608	  -- (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso); tensoring by `id` of an iso is flatness-free.
609	  --
610	  -- iter-214 CORRECTION (Step 0 + d.1 partial): the module-level stalk is NOT
611	  -- Mathlib-absent — `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk` supplies, for
612	  -- `X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module
613	  -- `Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` and `germ_smul`. The
614	  -- linear-stalk-map packaging (ingredient d.1) is now built project-side:
615	  -- `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap` (+ `stalkLinearMap_germ`,
616	  -- `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`), all axiom-clean.
617	  --
618	  -- The TWO residual gaps to close this sorry (both require SPECIALISING this lemma
619	  -- to the topological site `C = Opens X` — the general-site statement here has no
620	  -- stalks; decl is UNPROTECTED so the specialisation is free, and the only consumer
621	  -- `tensorObj_assoc_iso` already works over `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`):
622	  --   (d.1-bridge) `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W (toPresheaf-image) ↔ ∀ x,
623	  --     IsIso (stalkFunctor Ab x map)` — assemble from `HasEnoughPoints
624	  --     (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)` (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Points.lean`) +
625	  --     `W_iff`, OR from `WEqualsLocallyBijective` + the TopCat criteria
626	  --     `locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks` /
627	  --     `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`
628	  --     (needs `presheafFiber ≅ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk`, the Mathlib TODO bridge).
629	  --   (d.2) stalk ⊗ commutation `(F ⊗ᵖ M)_x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R_x} M_x` naturally identifying
630	  --     `(F ◁ g)_x` with `LinearMap.lTensor F_x (stalkLinearMap g x)` — "tensor
631	  --     commutes with the filtered colimit defining the stalk" over the varying ring;
632	  --     genuinely Mathlib-absent (largest piece). Then `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`
633	  --     + `LinearEquiv.lTensor` finish (flatness-free). See task result for full decomp.
634	  sorry
635	
636	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
637	ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
638	locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
639	Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
640	local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
641	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
642	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
643	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
644	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
645	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
646	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
647	  have hbij := hg
648	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
649	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
650	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
651	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
652	
653	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
654	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
655	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
656	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
657	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
658	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
659	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
660	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
661	  have hconj : g ▷ F
662	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
663	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
664	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
665	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
666	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
667	  exact hwl
668	
669	end WhiskerOfW
670	
671	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
672	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
673	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
674	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
675	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
676	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
677	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
678	read at a single morphism. -/
679	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
680	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
681	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
682	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
683	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
684	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
685	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
686	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
687	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
688	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
689	  exact h2
690	
691	end FlatWhisker

693	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
694	(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)
695	
696	The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
697	argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
698	id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
699	characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
700	Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
701	as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.
702	
703	Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
704	and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
705	`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
706	scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
707	linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
708	This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
709	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
710	`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/
711	
712	section StalkLinearMap
713	
714	open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite
715	
716	variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}
717	
718	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
719	For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
720	`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
721	((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
722	for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
723	Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
724	linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
725	stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
726	noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
727	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
728	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
729	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
730	  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
731	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
732	  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
733	  map_smul' r s := by
734	    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
735	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
736	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
737	    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
738	    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
739	    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
740	    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
741	    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
742	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
743	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
744	    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
745	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
746	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
747	      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
748	    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
749	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
750	            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
751	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
752	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
753	            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
754	      intro z
755	      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
756	            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
757	            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
758	      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
759	        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
760	    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
761	        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]
762	
763	/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
764	an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
765	defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
766	(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
767	lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
768	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
769	    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
770	    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
771	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
772	          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
773	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
774	        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
775	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
776	  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
777	        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
778	        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
779	  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
780	    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s
781	
782	/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
783	If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
784	g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
785	equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
786	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
787	equivalence, flatness-free). -/
788	lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
789	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
790	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
791	    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
792	  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
793	    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
794	  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _
795	
796	/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
797	underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
798	to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
799	`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
800	an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
801	noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
802	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
803	    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
804	    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
805	      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
806	  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)
807	
808	end StalkLinearMap
809	
810	end PresheafOfModules
811	
812	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the presheaf-level pushforward adjunction (H1)
813	
814	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.{pushforwardNatTrans, pushforwardCongr,
815	pushforwardPushforwardAdj}`
816	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean`, L154/L73/L226) to the
817	`PresheafOfModules` level. Every line of the sheaf template already manipulates
818	`.val`/`.val.presheaf` presheaf data, so the de-sheafification is mechanical (drop the `Sheaf`
819	wrapper and the sheaf-only `IsContinuous` `letI`s). These are the **H1** linchpin of
820	`tensorObj_restrict_iso`: from a pair `adj : F ⊣ G` one obtains a presheaf-level adjunction
821	`pushforward φ ⊣ pushforward ψ`, hence — against the existing
822	`PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` and via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` —
823	the iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ` that moves the abstract presheaf pullback onto the concrete
824	restriction pushforward. Per blueprint `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, Step 3 (the H1 residual). -/
825	
826	namespace PresheafOfModules
827	
828	open CategoryTheory Functor
829	
830	section PushforwardNatTrans
831	
832	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
833	
834	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
835	  {F G : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
836	  (φ : S ⟶ G.op ⋙ R)
837	
838	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardNatTrans`.** A natural transformation `α : F ⟶ G` of functors
839	`C ⥤ D` induces a natural transformation between the pushforward functors along `F` and `G`.
840	De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans`
841	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:154`), dropping the `.val` wrapper. -/
842	noncomputable def pushforwardNatTrans (α : F ⟶ G) :
843	    pushforward.{u} φ ⟶ pushforward.{u} (φ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op α) R) where
844	  app M :=
845	    { app := fun U => (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app U).hom).map (M.map (α.app U.unop).op)
846	      naturality := fun {U V} i => by
847	        ext x
848	        dsimp
849	        change (M.presheaf.map (G.map i.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (α.app V.unop).op) _ =
850	          (M.presheaf.map (α.app U.unop).op ≫ M.presheaf.map (F.map i.unop).op) _
851	        simp only [← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, α.naturality] }
852	  naturality := fun {M N} f => by
853	    ext U x
854	    exact congr($(f.naturality (α.app U.unop).op) x).symm
855	
856	@[simp] lemma pushforwardNatTrans_app_app_apply (α : F ⟶ G) (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R)
857	    (U : Cᵒᵖ) (x) :
858	    ((pushforwardNatTrans φ α).app M).app U x = M.map (α.app U.unop).op x := rfl
859	
860	end PushforwardNatTrans
861	
862	section PushforwardCongr
863	
864	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
865	
866	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
867	  {F : C ⥤ D} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
868	
869	/-- **Presheaf-level `pushforwardCongr`.** Pushforwards along equal morphisms of presheaves of
870	rings are isomorphic. De-sheafification of `SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr`
871	(`Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:73`), dropping the `fullyFaithfulForget` preimage (the
872	presheaf-level `pushforward` lands in `PresheafOfModules` directly). -/
873	noncomputable def pushforwardCongr {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ) :
874	    pushforward.{u} φ ≅ pushforward.{u} ψ :=
875	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun M ↦
876	    PresheafOfModules.isoMk
877	      (fun U ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr (R := S.obj U) (S := R.obj _)
878	        (f := (φ.app U).hom) (g := (ψ.app U).hom) (by subst e; rfl)).app _)
879	      (fun _ _ _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)) (fun _ ↦ by subst e; rfl)
880	
881	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
882	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
883	    ((pushforwardCongr e).hom.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
884	
885	@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {φ ψ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R} (e : φ = ψ)
886	    (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} R) (U x) :
887	    ((pushforwardCongr e).inv.app M).app U x = x := by subst e; rfl
888	
889	end PushforwardCongr
890	
891	section PushforwardAdj
892	
893	universe v₁ v₂ uC uD
894	
895	variable {C : Type uC} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type uD} [Category.{v₂} D]
896	  {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}

899	  (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ S)

901	  (H₂ : φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ ≫ Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op adj.unit) S = 𝟙 S)
